question_text: Which source backbone maps to U07 - Rehabilitative Nursing?
rationale: U07 is built from Module 14 content focused on rehabilitative nursing and restorative CNA support.

question_text: Resident G can wash the face and upper body after setup but asks the CNA to do everything because it is faster. What is the best restorative response?
answer_a: Complete the entire bath quickly so the resident does not have to try
answer_b: Set up supplies, provide privacy, cue the resident to do the parts they can safely do, and assist with the rest
answer_c: Tell the resident they are being lazy if they do not wash independently
answer_d: Leave the room and return after the resident completes all care alone
rationale: Restorative ADL support means setup, cueing, time, dignity, and the amount of help required by the care plan--not taking over or abandoning the resident.

question_text: During wheelchair setup, the CNA notices the cushion is missing and one footrest is loose. Which action is outside CNA scope?
answer_a: Stop the transfer setup until safe equipment or assistance is available
answer_b: Report the unsafe device concern to the nurse according to policy
answer_c: Change the mobility plan independently and discontinue the wheelchair device permanently
answer_d: Document the observed equipment issue and notification
rationale: The CNA reports and follows the care plan; independently changing a mobility or device plan is beyond CNA scope.

question_text: Which documentation entry is most objective for restorative bathing support?
answer_a: Resident was stubborn and made care take too long.
answer_b: Resident probably needs a different therapy plan.
answer_c: Family makes the resident too dependent.
answer_d: Resident washed face and upper body after setup; used long-handled sponge with two verbal cues; stated, 'I am tired'; nurse notified of fatigue.
rationale: Objective restorative documentation includes the task, assistance level, device/cueing, resident response or statement, and notification without labels or diagnosis.

question_text: What does restorative care mean in this unit?
answer_b: Care that supports the resident's highest practical level of function
answer_d: A replacement for physical therapy orders
rationale: Restorative care focuses on maintaining or improving the resident's function through care-plan guided daily support.

question_text: During assigned active-assistive range of motion, a resident grimaces and says, 'That hurts sharply in my shoulder.' What should the CNA do?
answer_a: Force the joint through the full range to prevent contracture
answer_b: Ignore the statement if the exercise was assigned
answer_c: Stop that movement, keep the resident safe, report the pain promptly, and document facts as assigned
answer_d: Increase repetitions so the shoulder becomes stronger
rationale: Sharp pain during assigned ROM is a reportable concern; the CNA should not force movement or change the exercise plan independently.

question_text: What evidence should Moodle retain for U07 completion?
answer_a: Only a verbal promise that the learner studied
answer_b: Clinical-hour attendance from a facility shift
answer_c: A copied source page with the learner's name
answer_d: Page/book completion, scenario attempt, reflection/attestation, quiz score, time/log data, and unit completion status
rationale: U07 completion depends on Moodle evidence of online engagement and active participation; it does not use clinical-hour proof or copied source pages.

question_text: A family member says, 'Just dress her yourself; it is painful to watch her struggle,' but the resident can dress with cueing. Which CNA response best protects dignity and restorative goals?
answer_a: Tell the family member to leave because they are interfering
answer_b: Dress the resident completely and do not mention the care plan
answer_c: Say the plan encourages the resident to do safe parts independently, provide cueing and help as needed, and refer detailed goal questions to the nurse or therapist
answer_d: Promise the resident will regain full independence if the family stops helping
rationale: The CNA can explain care-plan guided participation respectfully while preserving dignity and referring therapy-goal questions to licensed or therapy staff.

question_text: A resident who usually joins a walking program has stayed in bed most of the week and now needs more help standing. Which concern best connects to complications of inactivity?
answer_a: Inactivity has no effect if meals are delivered to the room
answer_b: The CNA should create a new strengthening plan without telling anyone
answer_c: The resident should be scolded for losing motivation
answer_d: Weakness, joint stiffness, pressure injury risk, constipation, lung congestion, and loss of confidence should be reported and addressed through the care plan
rationale: Inactivity can affect muscles, joints, skin, lungs, elimination, and self-esteem; the CNA reports changes and follows the restorative plan.

question_text: Which statement is compliance-safe for this Draft / Pending CDPH Approval online CE unit?
answer_a: Keep certificate release blocked until approval metadata, identifiers, required evidence, and administrative gates are satisfied
answer_b: Advertise the unit as CDPH approved because the learner passed the quiz
answer_c: Count this online lesson as hands-on ROM validation
answer_d: Ask learners to enter real resident details for realistic documentation practice
rationale: The course may provide online CE content while pending but must not claim approval or release certificates until approval and evidence requirements are met.
